mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound since March 2020, the persistent gap between near-zero policy rates and equity earnings yields structurally favors risk assets; sustained ultra-low rates mechanically compress the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate regime holds.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 at t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX exhibits negative median 20d returns during periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in the pace of unemployment rate decline (UNRATE improvement slowing after the initial post-shock recovery) signals that the labor market recovery is stalling, which historically precedes equity market pullbacks over the following month as growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month improvement decelerates to less than 0.3pp after two consecutive months of larger declines
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.9 at t-1mo, down from double-digit pandemic peak
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery deceleration phase
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following deceleration signals are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery is being financed by both discretionary spending and durable goods/housing investment; when both series rise together it signals a self-reinforcing demand expansion that supports equity valuations over a full quarter, whereas divergence between the two undermines the signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=549256 at t-2mo, HOUST=1415 at t-2mo showing simultaneous elevated levels
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST positive momentum fails to outperform single-series RSAFS-only signal on Rank IC by a margin greater than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) recovering back toward pre-pandemic levels after the 2020 collapse signals that supply-side capacity utilization is normalizing, which reduces uncertainty about corporate earnings and supports risk asset prices over a quarterly horizon, but only while the economy remains in a post-shock recovery regime rather than a mature expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.5055 at t-2mo, still below pre-pandemic peak but recovering
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month gains fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC above 0.03 during 2020-2021 recovery window, or the effect reverses once INDPRO surpasses pre-pandemic peak (mature cycle).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth is a near-real-time signal of labor market momentum that is quickly priced by equity markets within the following trading week, generating a short-horizon positive drift in SPX returns immediately after strong payroll prints.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=142373 thousand at t-1mo, showing continued monthly gains during recovery
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant difference (t-test p>0.05) between months with accelerating vs decelerating PAYEMS growth over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden upside surprise in CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation above a threshold signals that inflationary pressure is building faster than anticipated, raising the probability of future FEDFUNDS tightening and compressing equity valuation multiples, producing negative SPX returns over the following month once the threshold is breached.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=260.325 at t-1mo, FEDFUNDS=0.09 at t-1mo indicating low inflation currently but low policy buffer
regime_hint: low interest rate environment with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or neutral (median >= 0) in more than 55% of triggered high-inflation-surprise instances despite low FEDFUNDS regime.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth and monthly retail sales (RSAFS) captures both the backward-looking confirmation of aggregate output recovery and the forward-looking consumer spending pulse; when both indicators point to expansion simultaneously, the compounded demand signal drives S&P 500 gains over the following quarter more reliably than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=18583.984 billion at t-4mo reflecting Q3 2020 rebound, RSAFS=549256 at t-2mo showing elevated retail spending
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with confirmed GDP rebound
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1+RSAFS signal fails to improve Rank IC by more than 0.02 relative to RSAFS alone over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no genuine interaction beyond additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate regime, short-horizon rebounds in industrial production (INDPRO) are amplified by cheap financing conditions (FEDFUNDS near zero), producing an outsized one-week positive equity reaction to supply-side data surprises compared to what would occur in a higher-rate regime, because low funding costs let firms quickly monetize capacity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 at t-1mo and INDPRO=101.5055 at t-2mo recovering post-pandemic
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime combined with industrial recovery
failure_condition: The INDPRO-FEDFUNDS interaction signal fails to produce a stronger 5-day Rank IC than INDPRO alone by at least 0.02, or the positive effect disappears/reverses once FEDFUNDS rises above 1%.
